intake.event_framing: Assess how a major debate collapse changes the final month of a national election.
intake.current_development: A debate collapse forces both campaigns to reset strategy as party leadership scrambles to stabilize messaging.
known_unknowns: Whether swing voters interpret the event as a competency issue

summary: Turnout effects may matter more than persuasion

documents.debate-fallout.md: Party leadership scrambles to stabilize messaging after the debate while donor confidence softens.
documents.turnout-rescue.md: Campaign organizers launch a turnout rescue plan to keep early vote mobilization from collapsing.

case_title: South Africa 2024 coalition negotiations and Government of National Unity
historical_outcome: After losing its parliamentary majority, the ANC moved toward a Government of National Unity and cross-party dealmaking instead of trying to govern alone.

sources.title: Handover of Final List of Elected Representatives for National Assembly and Provincial Legislatures
ANC Welcomes Political Parties to the Government of National Unity

intake.event_framing: Assess coalition-shaping dynamics after South Africa's 2024 election produces no outright majority.
intake.current_development: The ANC loses its governing majority after the 2024 election and coalition negotiations intensify around a possible Government of National Unity.
known_constraints: No single party has a full governing mandate
Investors and voters want a stable governing arrangement quickly
known_unknowns: Whether coalition partners can align on a workable governing framework

summary: Coalition bargaining and elite dealmaking will matter more than fresh persuasion

documents.iec-results.md: South Africa's 2024 election produced a fragmented parliament with no single party able to govern alone and coalition talks becoming decisive.
documents.gnu-statement.md: The ANC said the election result required parties to work together and welcomed the formation of a Government of National Unity built around a statement of intent.

case_title: Germany 2021 election and traffic-light coalition formation
historical_outcome: After the 2021 election produced no outright majority, Germany moved toward a negotiated SPD-Greens-FDP traffic-light coalition rather than a simple messaging contest.

intake.event_framing: Assess coalition-shaping dynamics after Germany's 2021 federal election produces no single-party governing majority.
intake.current_development: Germany's 2021 election produces a fragmented parliament, SPD finishes first, and coalition bargaining shifts toward a possible traffic-light coalition with the Greens and FDP.
known_constraints: A workable governing coalition has to be assembled quickly enough to preserve stability
Party leaders need coalition arithmetic and message discipline to line up at the same time
known_unknowns: Whether coalition partners can align on a durable governing program

summary: Elite dealmaking and coalition bargaining matter more than fresh persuasion at this stage

documents.bundestag-result.md: Germany's 2021 federal election produced no outright majority and left coalition formation dependent on post-election bargaining.
documents.traffic-light-summary.md: The Federal Government later described the first federal traffic-light coalition as the outcome of that bargaining process.

case_title: UK 2017 hung parliament and DUP confidence-and-supply deal
historical_outcome: The 2017 UK election produced a hung parliament that ended in a negotiated Conservative-DUP confidence-and-supply deal rather than an immediate fresh election.

sources.title: Results and turnout at the 2017 UK general election
PM statement on Confidence and Supply Agreement with the DUP

intake.event_framing: Assess coalition-shaping dynamics after the 2017 UK general election produces a hung parliament.
intake.current_development: The 2017 UK election produces a hung parliament and pushes the Conservatives toward confidence-and-supply negotiations with the DUP.
known_constraints: The government needs a workable parliamentary arrangement quickly
Party leaders must stabilize confidence without reopening the campaign immediately
known_unknowns: Whether a support arrangement can be secured without a broader coalition deal

summary: Parliamentary bargaining and elite negotiation now matter more than mass persuasion

documents.hung-parliament.md: The 2017 UK general election produced a hung parliament, forcing immediate bargaining over who could command confidence in the Commons.
documents.dup-agreement.md: Downing Street said a confidence-and-supply agreement had been made with the DUP to support the government on key votes.

case_title: Dutch coalition negotiations leading to the Schoof government
historical_outcome: The Dutch post-election shock resolved through a negotiated coalition that produced the Schoof government, not through a simple winner-take-all narrative fight.

sources.title: Government statement of policy on taking office, 3 July 2024

intake.event_framing: Assess coalition-shaping dynamics after Dutch election fragmentation leads to the 2024 Schoof government.
intake.current_development: After prolonged negotiations following the Dutch election, coalition partners move toward a government led by Dick Schoof with multiple parties sharing cabinet control.
known_constraints: The coalition has to convert a fragmented result into a workable governing arrangement
Public and investor confidence depend on a stable government taking office
known_unknowns: Whether the parties can maintain enough discipline to launch the coalition successfully

summary: Coalition agreement mechanics and elite bargaining dominate over fresh persuasion at this stage

documents.schoof-swearing-in.md: The Dutch government said the Schoof government was sworn in as a coalition of PVV, VVD, NSC, and BBB.
documents.policy-statement.md: The new Dutch government's statement of policy tied its program to the coalition agreement reached after the election negotiations.
